rationale: The actual resolution was a terse duplicate-closure pointing to #4808/#4809, and X's final recommendation ('keep closed as duplicate') matches that outcome while appropriately hedging on what it couldn't verify (main's actual state) and adding a concrete, useful follow-up (test coverage gap in _STALE_API_FILES). Y instead asserts 'already fixed on main' as verified fact, which isn't what the ground truth shows, and contains an internal contradiction — first stating #4810 fixed only the AR-side loader (DiT missed), then later claiming #4810 'landed the broader fix for all five affected loaders,' which conflicts with the issue's own premise. Both cite plausible file/line evidence, but X's epistemic hedging is better calibrated to the genuinely uncertain merge status, making it more trustworthy and complete for the reporter's actual crash.
